mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the median forward return when UNRATE < 5.0% is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion combined with rising industrial production signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, driving positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or periods of high RSAFS and high INDPRO yield negative median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if HOUST increases do not correlate with positive 5-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or if the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity, directly supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls decline for two consecutive months, it signals labor market deterioration that typically precedes economic slowdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the median forward return when PAYEMS declines consecutively is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit coupled with stable inflation indicates improving household purchasing power without triggering rate hikes, driving positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or periods of high RSAFS and low CPIAUCSL yield negative median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth acts as a fundamental earnings driver, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days specifically when the economy transitions out of a recessionary phase.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if GDPC1 acceleration does not correlate with positive 5-day forward returns during post-recession periods, or if the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window.
